objective: Maximize the probability of a valid resolution or externally useful contribution per unit of compute and model effort; do not optimize for activity, one method's closure rate, or the elegance of one route.
standing_question: What is the easiest honest path to settle or materially advance this exact problem now?
required_resolution_paths: structural lemma or reduction shrinking either route
relevant transfer methods from adjacent fields
required_path_assessment: whether success directly settles the target
selection_rule: At selection and after every substantive result, score all materially distinct paths by expected marginal value. Give constructive and negative routes symmetric consideration, retain comparable routes, alternate near-tied families, and allocate the next bounded tranche to the route expected to remove the most remaining uncertainty per CPU-hour and model effort.
switch_rule: Stop, hold, or demote a route when current evidence makes another materially superior. Momentum, prior engineering investment, and artifact volume are not reasons to continue.
experiment_rule: Use short, predeclared, cheap discriminators before scale-up. A method remains eligible when it creates net-new validated progress; it need not dominate every case.
verification_rule: Directly and independently validate witnesses; reconstruct and replay UNSAT claims; maintain a hash-bound uncertainty or frontier manifest; distinguish local results from global theorems; and recheck prior art and novelty before promotion.
